<<The function will>> <<traverse the list>> <<of tuples recursively,>> <<checking each element>> <<for numeric type.>> <<If an element is>> <<a tuple, it will>> <<process it further.>> <<If it is a number,>> <<it will be added>> <<to the cumulative>> <<sum. Non-numeric>> <<values are skipped.>> <<This approach ensures>> <<that even deeply>> <<nested tuples are>> <<handled efficiently.>> <<The function will>> <<return the total>> <<sum of all numeric>> <<values found in the>> <<input structure.>>

<<An alternative>> <<implementation could>> <<use iteration and>> <<a stack to simulate>> <<recursion, avoiding>> <<potential recursion>> <<depth issues.>> <<Each tuple is>> <<pushed onto the>> <<stack, and elements>> <<are processed one>> <<by one. Numeric>> <<values are summed,>> <<and non-numeric>> <<elements are ignored.>> <<This method is>> <<more memory-efficient>> <<for very large>> <<structures. Both>> <<approaches achieve>> <<the same goal but>> <<differ in their>> <<implementation.
